469	fn anyhow_to_pliron(e: anyhow::Error) -> pliron::result::Error {
470	    pliron::create_error!(
471	        pliron::location::Location::Unknown,
472	        pliron::result::ErrorKind::VerificationFailed,
473	        pliron::result::StringError(e.to_string())
474	    )
475	}

477	/// Convert `mir.call` to `llvm.call` with argument flattening.
478	///
479	/// Performs ABI-level transformations to match CUDA calling conventions:
480	/// - Slice arguments: flattened to `(ptr, len)` pairs
481	/// - Struct arguments: flattened to individual fields
482	/// - Unit return type: converted to void
483	/// - Callee name: `::` mangled to `__` for LLVM identifier validity
484	/// - Device extern calls: prefix stripped to use original symbol name
485	pub fn convert(
486	    ctx: &mut Context,
487	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
488	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
489	    operands_info: &OperandsInfo,
490	) -> Result<()> {
491	    let callee_name: String = {
492	        let mir_call = MirCallOp::new(op);
493	        let callee_attr = match mir_call.get_attr_callee(ctx) {
494	            Some(a) => a,
495	            None => {
496	                return pliron::input_err!(
497	                    op.deref(ctx).loc(),
498	                    "MirCallOp missing callee attribute"
499	                );
500	            }
501	        };
502	        (*callee_attr).clone().into()
503	    };
504	
505	    if let Some(intrinsic) = RustBitIntrinsic::from_placeholder_callee(&callee_name) {
506	        return convert_rust_bit_intrinsic(ctx, rewriter, op, intrinsic);
507	    }
508	
509	    if let Some(intrinsic) = RustSaturatingIntrinsic::from_placeholder_callee(&callee_name) {
510	        return convert_rust_saturating_intrinsic(ctx, rewriter, op, operands_info, intrinsic);
511	    }
512	
513	    if let Some(RustBigIntIntrinsic::CarryingMulAdd) =
514	        RustBigIntIntrinsic::from_placeholder_callee(&callee_name)
515	    {
516	        return convert_rust_carrying_mul_add(ctx, rewriter, op, operands_info);
517	    }
518	
519	    if let Some(intrinsic) = RustFloatMathIntrinsic::from_placeholder_callee(&callee_name) {
520	        return convert_rust_float_math_intrinsic(ctx, rewriter, op, intrinsic);
521	    }
522	
523	    let callee_ident: pliron::identifier::Identifier = {
524	        let resolved_name = resolve_device_extern_symbol(&callee_name);
525	
526	        resolved_name
527	            .try_into()
528	            .expect("callee name should have been legalized during MIR import")
529	    };
530	
531	    let args: Vec<Value> = op.deref(ctx).operands().collect();
532	
533	    let has_result = op.deref(ctx).get_num_results() > 0;
534	    let mir_result_ty_ptr = if has_result {
535	        Some(op.deref(ctx).get_result(0).get_type(ctx))
536	    } else {
537	        None
538	    };
539	
540	    let mut zst_replacement_type = None;
541	    let result_type = if let Some(mir_ty) = mir_result_ty_ptr {
542	        // Only the empty tuple `()` is the unit type. `is::<MirTupleType>()`
543	        // also matches `(T, U, ...)`, so we have to peek at the field count.
544	        // Non-empty tuples take the convert_type path and end up as an LLVM
545	        // struct return, same as named structs.
546	        let is_unit = mir_ty
547	            .deref(ctx)
548	            .downcast_ref::<MirTupleType>()
549	            .is_some_and(|t| t.get_types().is_empty());
550	        let converted = convert_type(ctx, mir_ty).map_err(anyhow_to_pliron)?;
551	        if is_unit || is_zero_sized_type(ctx, converted) {
552	            // NVPTX cannot carry a ZST in a function signature, so the call
553	            // itself returns void. Keep the converted ZST type so any MIR uses
554	            // of the result can be replaced with a typed undef below.
555	            zst_replacement_type = Some(converted);
556	            llvm_types::VoidType::get(ctx).into()
557	        } else {
558	            converted
559	        }
560	    } else {
561	        llvm_types::VoidType::get(ctx).into()
562	    };
563	
564	    // Look up the callee's declared signature in the parent module. The
565	    // declaration may already have been lowered to `llvm::FuncOp` (typical
566	    // for device-extern decls inserted by the importer ahead of time, and
567	    // for callees whose conversion ran first), or it may still be a
568	    // `MirFuncOp` whose body hasn't been touched yet. In the second case
569	    // we run the same MIR-to-LLVM signature flattening that the function
570	    // converter will eventually apply, so the call site sees the exact
571	    // same parameter types the callee will be lowered to. This makes
572	    // intra-Rust calls into shared-memory-typed params and device-extern
573	    // calls with the generic ABI work uniformly.
574	    let callee_decl_arg_types = find_callee_arg_types(ctx, op, &callee_ident).unwrap_or_default();
575	    let expected_param_tys = if callee_decl_arg_types.is_empty() {
576	        None
577	    } else {
578	        Some(callee_decl_arg_types.as_slice())
579	    };
580	
581	    let (flattened_args, flattened_arg_types) =
582	        flatten_arguments(ctx, rewriter, &args, operands_info, expected_param_tys)?;
583	
584	    let func_type = llvm_types::FuncType::get(ctx, result_type, flattened_arg_types, false);
585	
586	    // Direct libdevice calls (`__nv_*`) originate from hand-written externs
587	    // (e.g. khal_std's `Float::asin`/`acos` → `__nv_asinf`/`__nv_acosf`) that
588	    // have no MIR body and therefore no emitted declaration. Without a module
589	    // declaration the verifier rejects the call ("Symbol __nv_asinf not found").
590	    // Declare it here with the call's signature, mirroring the float-math
591	    // intrinsic path; the symbol is bound against libdevice at the cubin-link
592	    // stage just like `__nv_expf`/`__nv_sqrtf`.
593	    {
594	        let resolved_name = resolve_device_extern_symbol(&callee_name);
595	        let parent_block = op.deref(ctx).get_parent_block();
596	        if resolved_name.starts_with("__nv_")
597	            && let Some(parent_block) = parent_block
598	        {
599	            let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
600	            helpers::ensure_intrinsic_declared(ctx, parent_block, &resolved_name, func_type)
601	                .map_err(|e| {
602	                    pliron::input_error!(
603	                        loc,
604	                        "Failed to declare libdevice extern {resolved_name}: {e}"
605	                    )
606	                })?;
607	        }
608	    }
609	
610	    let llvm_call = llvm::CallOp::new(
611	        ctx,
612	        CallOpCallable::Direct(callee_ident),
613	        func_type,
614	        flattened_args,
615	    );
616	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, llvm_call.get_operation());
617	
618	    let is_void = result_type.deref(ctx).is::<llvm_types::VoidType>();
619	    if has_result && !is_void && llvm_call.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_num_results() > 0 {
620	        rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, llvm_call.get_operation());
621	    } else if op.deref(ctx).has_use()
622	        && let Some(zst_type) = zst_replacement_type
623	    {
624	        // The ABI intentionally erases ZST returns, but MIR may still use the
625	        // typed result (return it again, pass it to another call, or project a
626	        // zero-sized field). Preserve the side-effecting void call and replace
627	        // only its value result with an undef of the converted ZST type.
628	        let undef = llvm::UndefOp::new(ctx, zst_type);
629	        rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, undef.get_operation());
630	        rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, undef.get_operation());
631	    } else {
632	        // The LLVM call has no usable result so the MIR op must be erased.
633	        // This is safe for a result-less call or an unused ZST result. Any
634	        // other live result means the result-type computation silently
635	        // dropped a real value; report that instead of letting pliron's
636	        // erase-with-uses invariant panic and escape into rustc as an ICE.
637	        if op.deref(ctx).has_use() {
638	            let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
639	            return pliron::input_err!(
640	                loc,
641	                "mir.call lowering produced a void LLVM call but the MIR op \
642	                 still has live uses; the return type was likely misclassified \
643	                 (for example, a non-unit tuple treated as `()`)"
644	            );
645	        }
646	        rewriter.erase_operation(ctx, op);
647	    }
648	
649	    Ok(())
650	}
651	
652	/// Lower placeholder calls for rustc's integer bit intrinsics to LLVM intrinsics.
653	///
654	/// Rust methods like `u128::rotate_left` call `core::intrinsics::rotate_left`
655	/// in libcore. The importer preserves that as a placeholder `mir.call`; here we
656	/// recover the concrete integer width and emit the corresponding overloaded
657	/// LLVM intrinsic.
658	fn convert_rust_bit_intrinsic(
659	    ctx: &mut Context,
660	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
661	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
662	    intrinsic: RustBitIntrinsic,
663	) -> Result<()> {
664	    let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
665	    if op.deref(ctx).get_num_results() != 1 {
666	        return pliron::input_err!(loc, "Rust bit intrinsic call must have one result");
667	    }
668	
669	    let args: Vec<Value> = op.deref(ctx).operands().collect();
670	    let Some(&value) = args.first() else {
671	        return pliron::input_err!(loc, "Rust bit intrinsic call missing integer operand");
672	    };
673	
674	    let value_ty = value.get_type(ctx);
675	    let value_width = integer_bit_width(ctx, value_ty, loc.clone())?;
676	    let mir_result_ty = op.deref(ctx).get_result(0).get_type(ctx);
677	    let result_type = convert_type(ctx, mir_result_ty).map_err(anyhow_to_pliron)?;
678	
679	    if matches!(intrinsic, RustBitIntrinsic::Bswap) && value_width == 8 {
680	        // LLVM has no useful byte swap for a single byte; Rust's semantics are identity.
681	        let bitcast = llvm::BitcastOp::new(ctx, value, result_type);
682	        rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, bitcast.get_operation());
683	        rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, bitcast.get_operation());
684	        return Ok(());
685	    }
686	
687	    let (intrinsic_name, intrinsic_args, intrinsic_result_ty) = match intrinsic {
688	        RustBitIntrinsic::RotateLeft | RustBitIntrinsic::RotateRight => {
689	            if args.len() != 2 {
690	                return pliron::input_err!(
691	                    loc,
692	                    "rotate intrinsic requires value and shift operands"
693	                );
694	            }
695	            let (shift, _) =
696	                cast_integer_value_to_type(ctx, rewriter, args[1], value_ty, loc.clone())?;
697	            let suffix = match intrinsic {
698	                RustBitIntrinsic::RotateLeft => "fshl",
699	                RustBitIntrinsic::RotateRight => "fshr",
700	                _ => unreachable!(),
701	            };
702	            (
703	                format!("llvm_{suffix}_i{value_width}"),
704	                vec![value, value, shift],
705	                value_ty,
706	            )
707	        }
708	        RustBitIntrinsic::Ctpop => (format!("llvm_ctpop_i{value_width}"), vec![value], value_ty),
709	        RustBitIntrinsic::Ctlz { zero_undef } => {
710	            let zero_undef = create_i1_constant(ctx, rewriter, zero_undef);
711	            (
712	                format!("llvm_ctlz_i{value_width}"),
713	                vec![value, zero_undef],
714	                value_ty,
715	            )
716	        }
717	        RustBitIntrinsic::Cttz { zero_undef } => {
718	            let zero_undef = create_i1_constant(ctx, rewriter, zero_undef);
719	            (
720	                format!("llvm_cttz_i{value_width}"),
721	                vec![value, zero_undef],
722	                value_ty,
723	            )
724	        }
725	        RustBitIntrinsic::Bswap => (format!("llvm_bswap_i{value_width}"), vec![value], value_ty),
726	        RustBitIntrinsic::Bitreverse => (
727	            format!("llvm_bitreverse_i{value_width}"),
728	            vec![value],
729	            value_ty,
730	        ),
731	    };
732	
733	    let arg_types = intrinsic_args
734	        .iter()
735	        .map(|arg| arg.get_type(ctx))
736	        .collect::<Vec<_>>();
737	    let func_ty = llvm_types::FuncType::get(ctx, intrinsic_result_ty, arg_types, false);
738	    let parent_block = op.deref(ctx).get_parent_block().ok_or_else(|| {
739	        pliron::input_error!(loc.clone(), "Rust bit intrinsic call has no parent block")
740	    })?;
741	    helpers::ensure_intrinsic_declared(ctx, parent_block, &intrinsic_name, func_ty)
742	        .map_err(|e| pliron::input_error!(loc.clone(), "Failed to declare intrinsic: {e}"))?;
743	
744	    let sym_name: pliron::identifier::Identifier = intrinsic_name
745	        .as_str()
746	        .try_into()
747	        .map_err(|e| pliron::input_error!(loc.clone(), "Invalid intrinsic name: {:?}", e))?;
748	    let llvm_call = llvm::CallOp::new(
749	        ctx,
750	        CallOpCallable::Direct(sym_name),
751	        func_ty,
752	        intrinsic_args,
753	    );
754	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, llvm_call.get_operation());
755	
756	    let call_result = llvm_call.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
757	    let (_, final_op) =
758	        cast_integer_value_to_type(ctx, rewriter, call_result, result_type, loc.clone())?;
759	    let replacement = final_op.unwrap_or_else(|| llvm_call.get_operation());
760	    rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, replacement);
761	
762	    Ok(())
763	}
764	
765	/// Lower placeholder calls for rustc's saturating integer intrinsics.
766	///
767	/// Rust preserves signedness in the original MIR type. The converted LLVM value
768	/// is signless, so this uses `operands_info` to choose `sadd/ssub` versus
769	/// `uadd/usub`.
770	fn convert_rust_saturating_intrinsic(
771	    ctx: &mut Context,
772	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
773	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
774	    operands_info: &OperandsInfo,
775	    intrinsic: RustSaturatingIntrinsic,
776	) -> Result<()> {
777	    let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
778	    if op.deref(ctx).get_num_results() != 1 {
779	        return pliron::input_err!(loc, "Rust saturating intrinsic call must have one result");
780	    }
781	
782	    let args: Vec<Value> = op.deref(ctx).operands().collect();
783	    if args.len() != 2 {
784	        return pliron::input_err!(
785	            loc,
786	            "Rust saturating intrinsic requires left and right operands"
787	        );
788	    }
789	
790	    let lhs = args[0];
791	    let rhs = args[1];
792	    let lhs_ty = lhs.get_type(ctx);
793	    let width = integer_bit_width(ctx, lhs_ty, loc.clone())?;
794	    let is_signed =
795	        if let Some(int_ty) = operands_info.lookup_most_recent_of_type::<IntegerType>(ctx, lhs) {
796	            int_ty.signedness() == Signedness::Signed
797	        } else {
798	            return pliron::input_err!(loc, "expected integer type for Rust saturating intrinsic");
799	        };
800	
801	    let (rhs, _) = cast_integer_value_to_type(ctx, rewriter, rhs, lhs_ty, loc.clone())?;
802	    let op_stem = match (is_signed, intrinsic) {
803	        (true, RustSaturatingIntrinsic::Add) => "sadd",
804	        (false, RustSaturatingIntrinsic::Add) => "uadd",
805	        (true, RustSaturatingIntrinsic::Sub) => "ssub",
806	        (false, RustSaturatingIntrinsic::Sub) => "usub",
807	    };
808	    let intrinsic_name = format!("llvm_{op_stem}_sat_i{width}");
809	    let func_ty = llvm_types::FuncType::get(ctx, lhs_ty, vec![lhs_ty, lhs_ty], false);
810	    let parent_block = op.deref(ctx).get_parent_block().ok_or_else(|| {
811	        pliron::input_error!(
812	            loc.clone(),
813	            "Rust saturating intrinsic call has no parent block"
814	        )
815	    })?;
816	    helpers::ensure_intrinsic_declared(ctx, parent_block, &intrinsic_name, func_ty)
817	        .map_err(|e| pliron::input_error!(loc.clone(), "Failed to declare intrinsic: {e}"))?;
818	
819	    let sym_name: pliron::identifier::Identifier = intrinsic_name
820	        .as_str()
821	        .try_into()
822	        .map_err(|e| pliron::input_error!(loc.clone(), "Invalid intrinsic name: {:?}", e))?;
823	    let llvm_call = llvm::CallOp::new(
824	        ctx,
825	        CallOpCallable::Direct(sym_name),
826	        func_ty,
827	        vec![lhs, rhs],
828	    );
829	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, llvm_call.get_operation());
830	
831	    let result_mir_ty = op.deref(ctx).get_result(0).get_type(ctx);
832	    let result_ty = convert_type(ctx, result_mir_ty).map_err(anyhow_to_pliron)?;
833	    let call_result = llvm_call.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
834	    let (_, final_op) =
835	        cast_integer_value_to_type(ctx, rewriter, call_result, result_ty, loc.clone())?;
836	    let replacement = final_op.unwrap_or_else(|| llvm_call.get_operation());
837	    rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, replacement);
838	
839	    Ok(())
840	}

842	/// Lower the placeholder call for rustc's `carrying_mul_add` bigint intrinsic.
843	///
844	/// `core::intrinsics::carrying_mul_add(a, b, c, d)` computes `a * b + c + d`
845	/// without losing any bits and returns the exact result split into a
846	/// `(low_half, high_half)` tuple. The integer methods `carrying_mul_add`,
847	/// `carrying_mul`, and `widening_mul` all funnel into this one intrinsic.
848	/// An N-bit multiply-accumulate always fits in 2*N bits:
849	/// even for the largest unsigned inputs,
850	/// `(2^N - 1)^2 + 2 * (2^N - 1) == 2^(2N) - 1`.
851	///
852	/// The lowering widens all four operands to 2*N bits (zero-extending for
853	/// unsigned types, sign-extending for signed types, matching the `as` casts
854	/// in core's fallback implementation), computes the product-sum in 2*N-bit
855	/// arithmetic, and splits the wide value:
856	///
857	/// ```text
858	/// wide = ext(a) * ext(b) + ext(c) + ext(d)  : i2N
859	/// low  = trunc(wide)                        : iN
860	/// high = trunc(wide >> N)                   : iN
861	/// result = { low, high }
862	/// ```
863	///
864	/// A logical shift (`lshr`) is used for the high half even for signed types:
865	/// core's fallback uses an arithmetic shift there, but the shifted value is
866	/// immediately truncated to N bits, and bits [N, 2N) of the wide value are
867	/// identical under either shift. The NVPTX backend pattern-matches this
868	/// ext/mul/shift idiom into `mul.lo` / `mul.hi` / `mad` instructions, so the
869	/// generated PTX is good.
870	///
871	/// 128-bit integers are rejected with a diagnostic: their lowering would
872	/// need 256-bit intermediate arithmetic, which NVPTX cannot legalize.
873	fn convert_rust_carrying_mul_add(
874	    ctx: &mut Context,
875	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
876	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
877	    operands_info: &OperandsInfo,
878	) -> Result<()> {
879	    let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
880	    if op.deref(ctx).get_num_results() != 1 {
881	        return pliron::input_err!(loc, "Rust carrying_mul_add intrinsic must have one result");
882	    }
883	
884	    let args: Vec<Value> = op.deref(ctx).operands().collect();
885	    if args.len() != 4 {
886	        return pliron::input_err!(
887	            loc,
888	            "Rust carrying_mul_add intrinsic requires four operands \
889	             (multiplier, multiplicand, addend, carry)"
890	        );
891	    }
892	
893	    let elem_ty = args[0].get_type(ctx);
894	    let width = integer_bit_width(ctx, elem_ty, loc.clone())?;
895	    if width > 64 {
896	        return pliron::input_err!(
897	            loc,
898	            "carrying_mul_add on {width}-bit integers is not yet supported on the device: \
899	             the lowering needs {}-bit intermediate arithmetic, which NVPTX cannot legalize",
900	            width * 2
901	        );
902	    }
903	
904	    // Rust preserves signedness in the original MIR type; the converted LLVM
905	    // value is signless, so recover it from the pre-conversion operand type.
906	    let is_signed = if let Some(int_ty) =
907	        operands_info.lookup_most_recent_of_type::<IntegerType>(ctx, args[0])
908	    {
909	        int_ty.signedness() == Signedness::Signed
910	    } else {
911	        return pliron::input_err!(
912	            loc,
913	            "expected integer type for Rust carrying_mul_add intrinsic"
914	        );
915	    };
916	
917	    let wide_ty: TypeHandle = IntegerType::get(ctx, width * 2, Signedness::Signless).into();
918	
919	    // Widen every operand to 2*N bits with the signedness-appropriate extension.
920	    let mut wide_args = Vec::with_capacity(4);
921	    for &arg in &args {
922	        let ext_op = if is_signed {
923	            llvm::SExtOp::new(ctx, arg, wide_ty).get_operation()
924	        } else {
925	            // `nneg` is a poison-introducing optimization flag ("the operand
926	            // is known non-negative"); we assert nothing and leave it unset.
927	            llvm::ZExtOp::new_with_nneg(ctx, arg, wide_ty, false).get_operation()
928	        };
929	        rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, ext_op);
930	        wide_args.push(ext_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0));
931	    }
932	
933	    // wide = a * b + c + d, computed in 2*N bits (cannot overflow).
934	    let flags = IntegerOverflowFlagsAttr::default();
935	    let mul = llvm::MulOp::new_with_overflow_flag(ctx, wide_args[0], wide_args[1], flags.clone())
936	        .get_operation();
937	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, mul);
938	    let product = mul.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
939	    let add_c = llvm::AddOp::new_with_overflow_flag(ctx, product, wide_args[2], flags.clone())
940	        .get_operation();
941	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, add_c);
942	    let sum_c = add_c.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
943	    let add_d =
944	        llvm::AddOp::new_with_overflow_flag(ctx, sum_c, wide_args[3], flags).get_operation();
945	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, add_d);
946	    let wide = add_d.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
947	
948	    // low = trunc(wide); high = trunc(wide >> N).
949	    let low_op = llvm::TruncOp::new(ctx, wide, elem_ty).get_operation();
950	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, low_op);
951	    let low = low_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
952	
953	    let shift_amount = {
954	        let wide_width = NonZeroUsize::new((width * 2) as usize).expect("width is non-zero");
955	        let attr = IntegerAttr::new(
956	            IntegerType::get(ctx, width * 2, Signedness::Signless),
957	            APInt::from_u64(u64::from(width), wide_width),
958	        );
959	        let const_op = llvm::ConstantOp::new(ctx, attr.into());
960	        rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, const_op.get_operation());
961	        const_op.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0)
962	    };
963	    let shr = llvm::LShrOp::new(ctx, wide, shift_amount).get_operation();
964	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, shr);
965	    let shifted = shr.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
966	    let high_op = llvm::TruncOp::new(ctx, shifted, elem_ty).get_operation();
967	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, high_op);
968	    let high = high_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
969	
970	    // Pack the (low, high) tuple into the converted result struct.
971	    let result_mir_ty = op.deref(ctx).get_result(0).get_type(ctx);
972	    let result_ty = convert_type(ctx, result_mir_ty).map_err(anyhow_to_pliron)?;
973	    let undef = llvm::UndefOp::new(ctx, result_ty);
974	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, undef.get_operation());
975	    let struct_val = undef.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
976	
977	    let insert_low = llvm::InsertValueOp::new(ctx, struct_val, low, vec![0]);
978	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, insert_low.get_operation());
979	    let struct_with_low = insert_low.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
980	
981	    let insert_high = llvm::InsertValueOp::new(ctx, struct_with_low, high, vec![1]);
982	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, insert_high.get_operation());
983	
984	    rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, insert_high.get_operation());
985	    Ok(())
986	}
987	
988	/// Lower placeholder calls for rustc's `f32` / `f64` math intrinsics to libdevice.
989	fn convert_rust_float_math_intrinsic(
990	    ctx: &mut Context,
991	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
992	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
993	    intrinsic: RustFloatMathIntrinsic,
994	) -> Result<()> {
995	    let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
996	    if op.deref(ctx).get_num_results() != 1 {
997	        return pliron::input_err!(loc, "Rust float math intrinsic call must have one result");
998	    }
999	
1000	    let args: Vec<Value> = op.deref(ctx).operands().collect();
1001	    let expected_args = intrinsic.arg_count();
1002	    if args.len() != expected_args {
1003	        return pliron::input_err!(
1004	            loc,
1005	            "Rust float math intrinsic requires {expected_args} operand(s)"
1006	        );
1007	    }
1008	
1009	    // `f*_fast` intrinsics lower to LLVM float binops with fast-math flags,
1010	    // not to a libdevice call. Route them off the libdevice path before any
1011	    // intrinsic-name lookup so polymorphic-over-T arithmetic on `f32` and
1012	    // `f64` both work without per-type intrinsic dispatch.
1013	    if let Some(binop) = intrinsic.fast_binop() {
1014	        return lower_fast_binop(ctx, rewriter, op, &args, binop, loc);
1015	    }
1016	
1017	    let result_mir_ty = op.deref(ctx).get_result(0).get_type(ctx);
1018	    let result_ty = convert_type(ctx, result_mir_ty).map_err(anyhow_to_pliron)?;
1019	    let intrinsic_name = intrinsic.libdevice_name(ctx, result_ty, loc.clone())?;
1020	    let arg_types = args.iter().map(|arg| arg.get_type(ctx)).collect::<Vec<_>>();
1021	    let func_ty = llvm_types::FuncType::get(ctx, result_ty, arg_types, false);
1022	    let parent_block = op.deref(ctx).get_parent_block().ok_or_else(|| {
1023	        pliron::input_error!(
1024	            loc.clone(),
1025	            "Rust float math intrinsic call has no parent block"
1026	        )
1027	    })?;
1028	    helpers::ensure_intrinsic_declared(ctx, parent_block, intrinsic_name, func_ty)
1029	        .map_err(|e| pliron::input_error!(loc.clone(), "Failed to declare intrinsic: {e}"))?;
1030	
1031	    let sym_name: pliron::identifier::Identifier = intrinsic_name
1032	        .try_into()
1033	        .map_err(|e| pliron::input_error!(loc.clone(), "Invalid intrinsic name: {:?}", e))?;
1034	    let llvm_call = llvm::CallOp::new(ctx, CallOpCallable::Direct(sym_name), func_ty, args);
1035	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, llvm_call.get_operation());
1036	    rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, llvm_call.get_operation());
1037	
1038	    Ok(())
1039	}
1040	
1041	/// Lower a `core::intrinsics::f*_fast` placeholder call to the matching
1042	/// LLVM float binop with fast-math flags.
1043	///
1044	/// The `f*_fast` family is generic over `T: FloatPrimitive`; rustc gives it
1045	/// no MIR body, so the importer emitted a placeholder `mir.call` and any
1046	/// downstream attempt to resolve the call as a symbol fails LLVM module
1047	/// verification. Replacing the call with a binop here gives LLVM the explicit
1048	/// `fast` fast-math flag set promised by the Rust intrinsic contract, so f32
1049	/// and f64 monomorphizations both work without per-type intrinsic dispatch.
1050	/// A compilation-wide no-FMA request removes only the contraction permission;
1051	/// all other finite-input relaxations remain intact.
1052	fn fast_float_intrinsic_flags(ctx: &Context) -> FastmathFlagsAttr {
1053	    // pliron-llvm's `FastmathFlagsAttr::default()` is `FastmathFlags::empty()`;
1054	    // `core::intrinsics::f*_fast` needs the explicit LLVM `fast` flag group.
1055	    let mut flags = FastmathFlags::FAST;
1056	    if !crate::context::lowering_options(ctx).allow_fma_contraction {
1057	        flags.remove(FastmathFlags::CONTRACT);
1058	    }
1059	    flags.into()
1060	}
1061	
1062	fn lower_fast_binop(
1063	    ctx: &mut Context,
1064	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
1065	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
1066	    args: &[Value],
1067	    binop: FastFloatBinop,
1068	    loc: pliron::location::Location,
1069	) -> Result<()> {
1070	    let (lhs, rhs) = match args {
1071	        [a, b] => (*a, *b),
1072	        _ => {
1073	            return pliron::input_err!(loc, "f*_fast intrinsic call must have exactly 2 operands");
1074	        }
1075	    };
1076	
1077	    let flags = fast_float_intrinsic_flags(ctx);
1078	    let llvm_op = match binop {
1079	        FastFloatBinop::Add => {
1080	            llvm::FAddOp::new_with_fast_math_flags(ctx, lhs, rhs, flags).get_operation()
1081	        }
1082	        FastFloatBinop::Sub => {
1083	            llvm::FSubOp::new_with_fast_math_flags(ctx, lhs, rhs, flags).get_operation()
1084	        }
1085	        FastFloatBinop::Mul => {
1086	            llvm::FMulOp::new_with_fast_math_flags(ctx, lhs, rhs, flags).get_operation()
1087	        }
1088	        FastFloatBinop::Div => {
1089	            llvm::FDivOp::new_with_fast_math_flags(ctx, lhs, rhs, flags).get_operation()
1090	        }
1091	        FastFloatBinop::Rem => {
1092	            llvm::FRemOp::new_with_fast_math_flags(ctx, lhs, rhs, flags).get_operation()
1093	        }
1094	    };
1095	
1096	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, llvm_op);
1097	    rewriter.replace_operation(ctx, op, llvm_op);
1098	    Ok(())
1099	}

1101	/// Read the width from an integer type, or report a useful lowering error.
1102	fn integer_bit_width(
1103	    ctx: &Context,
1104	    ty: TypeHandle,
1105	    loc: pliron::location::Location,
1106	) -> Result<u32> {
1107	    let ty_ref = ty.deref(ctx);
1108	    let Some(int_ty) = ty_ref.downcast_ref::<IntegerType>() else {
1109	        return pliron::input_err!(loc, "expected integer type for Rust bit intrinsic");
1110	    };
1111	    Ok(int_ty.width())
1112	}

1114	/// Return the libdevice `fabs` entry point for the concrete float type.
1115	fn fabs_libdevice_name(
1116	    ctx: &Context,
1117	    ty: TypeHandle,
1118	    loc: pliron::location::Location,
1119	) -> Result<&'static str> {
1120	    let ty_ref = ty.deref(ctx);
1121	    if ty_ref.is::<FP32Type>() {
1122	        Ok("__nv_fabsf")
1123	    } else if ty_ref.is::<FP64Type>() {
1124	        Ok("__nv_fabs")
1125	    } else {
1126	        pliron::input_err!(
1127	            loc,
1128	            "expected f32 or f64 type for Rust float math intrinsic"
1129	        )
1130	    }
1131	}

1133	/// Insert the `i1` flag operand used by `llvm.ctlz` and `llvm.cttz`.
1134	fn create_i1_constant(
1135	    ctx: &mut Context,
1136	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
1137	    value: bool,
1138	) -> Value {
1139	    let i1_ty = IntegerType::get(ctx, 1, Signedness::Signless);
1140	    let width = NonZeroUsize::new(1).expect("1 is non-zero");
1141	    let apint = APInt::from_u64(u64::from(value), width);
1142	    let attr = IntegerAttr::new(i1_ty, apint);
1143	    let const_op = llvm::ConstantOp::new(ctx, attr.into());
1144	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, const_op.get_operation());
1145	    const_op.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0)
1146	}
1147	
1148	/// Cast an integer value to the target width when Rust and LLVM disagree.
1149	///
1150	/// This is needed for count/zero intrinsics: LLVM returns `iN`, while Rust's
1151	/// public methods return `u32`.
1152	fn cast_integer_value_to_type(
1153	    ctx: &mut Context,
1154	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
1155	    value: Value,
1156	    target_ty: TypeHandle,
1157	    loc: pliron::location::Location,
1158	) -> Result<(Value, Option<Ptr<Operation>>)> {
1159	    let source_width = integer_bit_width(ctx, value.get_type(ctx), loc.clone())?;
1160	    let target_width = integer_bit_width(ctx, target_ty, loc)?;
1161	
1162	    if source_width == target_width {
1163	        return Ok((value, None));
1164	    }
1165	
1166	    let cast_op = if source_width < target_width {
1167	        let zext = llvm::ZExtOp::new(ctx, value, target_ty);
1168	        let nneg_key: pliron::identifier::Identifier = "llvm_nneg_flag".try_into().unwrap();
1169	        zext.get_operation()
1170	            .deref_mut(ctx)
1171	            .attributes
1172	            .set(nneg_key, pliron::builtin::attributes::BoolAttr::new(false));
1173	        zext.get_operation()
1174	    } else {
1175	        llvm::TruncOp::new(ctx, value, target_ty).get_operation()
1176	    };
1177	    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, cast_op);
1178	    Ok((cast_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0), Some(cast_op)))
1179	}
1180	
1181	/// Flatten arguments according to ABI rules and coerce each one to the
1182	/// callee's expected parameter type.
1183	///
1184	/// - Slice types → (ptr, len) pair
1185	/// - Struct types → individual field values (in MEMORY ORDER)
1186	/// - Other types → pass through
1187	///
1188	/// `expected_param_tys`, when present, is the LLVM-level (post-flatten)
1189	/// parameter signature of the callee. Each emitted (post-flatten) argument
1190	/// is coerced to its corresponding entry — in practice this means inserting
1191	/// an `addrspacecast` whenever a pointer arg's address space differs from
1192	/// the declared parameter's. When `expected_param_tys` is `None` (the
1193	/// callee declaration could not be located), each pointer falls back to
1194	/// being cast down to the generic address space, preserving the legacy
1195	/// behavior so device-extern calls (which always declare generic params)
1196	/// keep working.
1197	fn flatten_arguments(
1198	    ctx: &mut Context,
1199	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
1200	    args: &[Value],
1201	    operands_info: &OperandsInfo,
1202	    expected_param_tys: Option<&[TypeHandle]>,
1203	) -> Result<(Vec<Value>, Vec<TypeHandle>)> {
1204	    let mut flattened_args = Vec::new();
1205	    let mut flattened_arg_types = Vec::new();
1206	
1207	    // Helper: pull the next expected param type, if any.
1208	    let take_expected = |flattened_arg_types: &Vec<TypeHandle>| -> Option<TypeHandle> {
1209	        let idx = flattened_arg_types.len();
1210	        expected_param_tys.and_then(|tys| tys.get(idx).copied())
1211	    };
1212	
1213	    for arg in args.iter() {
1214	        let arg_ty = arg.get_type(ctx);
1215	
1216	        enum FlattenKind {
1217	            Slice,
1218	            Struct { layout: StructLayoutInfo },
1219	            None,
1220	        }
1221	
1222	        let flatten_kind = if let Some(mir_ty) = operands_info.lookup_most_recent_type(*arg) {
1223	            let ty_ref = mir_ty.deref(ctx);
1224	            if ty_ref.is::<MirSliceType>() || ty_ref.is::<MirDisjointSliceType>() {
1225	                FlattenKind::Slice
1226	            } else if let Some(struct_ty) = ty_ref.downcast_ref::<MirStructType>() {
1227	                FlattenKind::Struct {
1228	                    layout: StructLayoutInfo::of_struct(struct_ty),
1229	                }
1230	            } else {
1231	                FlattenKind::None
1232	            }
1233	        } else {
1234	            FlattenKind::None
1235	        };
1236	
1237	        match flatten_kind {
1238	            FlattenKind::Slice => {
1239	                let ptr_ty = llvm_types::PointerType::get_generic(ctx);
1240	                let len_ty = IntegerType::get(ctx, 64, Signedness::Signless);
1241	
1242	                let extract_ptr = llvm::ExtractValueOp::new(ctx, *arg, vec![0])?;
1243	                rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, extract_ptr.get_operation());
1244	                let ptr_val = extract_ptr.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1245	
1246	                let extract_len = llvm::ExtractValueOp::new(ctx, *arg, vec![1])?;
1247	                rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, extract_len.get_operation());
1248	                let len_val = extract_len.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1249	
1250	                let (ptr_val, ptr_ty) = coerce_arg_to_param_ty(
1251	                    ctx,
1252	                    rewriter,
1253	                    ptr_val,
1254	                    ptr_ty.into(),
1255	                    take_expected(&flattened_arg_types),
1256	                )?;
1257	                flattened_args.push(ptr_val);
1258	                flattened_arg_types.push(ptr_ty);
1259	
1260	                let (len_val, len_ty) = coerce_arg_to_param_ty(
1261	                    ctx,
1262	                    rewriter,
1263	                    len_val,
1264	                    len_ty.into(),
1265	                    take_expected(&flattened_arg_types),
1266	                )?;
1267	                flattened_args.push(len_val);
1268	                flattened_arg_types.push(len_ty);
1269	            }
1270	            FlattenKind::Struct { layout } => {
1271	                // Walk in memory order (the order `convert_function_type`
1272	                // flattens params in), extracting each non-ZST field from
1273	                // the slot the type converter placed it in, NOT from a
1274	                // running non-ZST count, which would land on `[N x i8]`
1275	                // padding slots for padded structs (issue #128).
1276	                let map = build_struct_slot_map(ctx, &layout).map_err(anyhow_to_pliron)?;
1277	                for &decl_idx in &layout.mem_to_decl {
1278	                    let Some(slot) = map.decl_to_llvm[decl_idx] else {
1279	                        continue; // ZST field: not passed.
1280	                    };
1281	                    let llvm_field_ty = map.field_llvm_types[decl_idx];
1282	                    let extract_op = llvm::ExtractValueOp::new(ctx, *arg, vec![slot])?;
1283	                    rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, extract_op.get_operation());
1284	                    let field_val = extract_op.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1285	
1286	                    let (field_val, field_ty) = coerce_arg_to_param_ty(
1287	                        ctx,
1288	                        rewriter,
1289	                        field_val,
1290	                        llvm_field_ty,
1291	                        take_expected(&flattened_arg_types),
1292	                    )?;
1293	                    flattened_args.push(field_val);
1294	                    flattened_arg_types.push(field_ty);
1295	                }
1296	            }
1297	            FlattenKind::None => {
1298	                if is_zero_sized_type(ctx, arg_ty) {
1299	                    continue;
1300	                }
1301	                let (final_arg, final_ty) = coerce_arg_to_param_ty(
1302	                    ctx,
1303	                    rewriter,
1304	                    *arg,
1305	                    arg_ty,
1306	                    take_expected(&flattened_arg_types),
1307	                )?;
1308	                flattened_args.push(final_arg);
1309	                flattened_arg_types.push(final_ty);
1310	            }
1311	        }
1312	    }
1313	
1314	    Ok((flattened_args, flattened_arg_types))
1315	}
1316	
1317	/// Coerce a single (post-flatten) argument so that it satisfies the callee's
1318	/// declared parameter type.
1319	///
1320	/// When `expected_ty` is supplied, this is the principled coercion used for
1321	/// every kind of call: pointer arguments whose address space differs from
1322	/// the declared parameter's are bridged with an `llvm.addrspacecast` to
1323	/// the declared address space (in either direction — generic ↔ shared,
1324	/// shared ↔ global, etc.). Non-pointer mismatches are left for the verifier
1325	/// to surface as a real type error.
1326	///
1327	/// When `expected_ty` is `None` (callee declaration not located), we fall
1328	/// back to the legacy "always generic" policy. That fallback exists because
1329	/// historically every call site cast pointer args down to addrspace(0) on
1330	/// the assumption that all callees declared their parameters as generic.
1331	/// Device-extern functions (`#[device] extern "C"`) still rely on that
1332	/// assumption and benefit from the fallback when their declaration hasn't
1333	/// landed in the module yet during conversion.
1334	fn coerce_arg_to_param_ty(
1335	    ctx: &mut Context,
1336	    rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
1337	    arg: Value,
1338	    arg_ty: TypeHandle,
1339	    expected_ty: Option<TypeHandle>,
1340	) -> Result<(Value, TypeHandle)> {
1341	    if let Some(expected_ty) = expected_ty {
1342	        if arg_ty == expected_ty {
1343	            return Ok((arg, arg_ty));
1344	        }
1345	        let arg_addrspace = pointer_addrspace(ctx, arg_ty);
1346	        let expected_addrspace = pointer_addrspace(ctx, expected_ty);
1347	        if let (Some(src_as), Some(dst_as)) = (arg_addrspace, expected_addrspace)
1348	            && src_as != dst_as
1349	        {
1350	            let cast_ty = llvm_types::PointerType::get(ctx, dst_as).into();
1351	            let cast_op = llvm::AddrSpaceCastOp::new(ctx, arg, cast_ty);
1352	            rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, cast_op.get_operation());
1353	            let casted_val = cast_op.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1354	            return Ok((casted_val, expected_ty));
1355	        }
1356	        return Ok((arg, arg_ty));
1357	    }
1358	
1359	    // Legacy fallback: cast any non-generic pointer down to addrspace(0).
1360	    let arg_addrspace = pointer_addrspace(ctx, arg_ty);
1361	    if let Some(addrspace) = arg_addrspace
1362	        && addrspace != ADDRSPACE_GENERIC
1363	    {
1364	        let cast_ty = llvm_types::PointerType::get(ctx, ADDRSPACE_GENERIC).into();
1365	        let cast_op = llvm::AddrSpaceCastOp::new(ctx, arg, cast_ty);
1366	        rewriter.insert_operation(ctx, cast_op.get_operation());
1367	        let casted_val = cast_op.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1368	        let generic_ptr_ty = llvm_types::PointerType::get_generic(ctx);
1369	        return Ok((casted_val, generic_ptr_ty.into()));
1370	    }
1371	
1372	    Ok((arg, arg_ty))
1373	}
1374	
1375	/// Return the address space of `ty` if it is an LLVM pointer type.
1376	fn pointer_addrspace(ctx: &Context, ty: TypeHandle) -> Option<u32> {
1377	    ty.deref(ctx)
1378	        .downcast_ref::<llvm_types::PointerType>()
1379	        .map(|ptr_ty| ptr_ty.address_space())
1380	}
1381	
1382	/// Look up the LLVM-level parameter signature of `callee_ident` by walking
1383	/// from `op` up to the parent module and scanning its top-level function
1384	/// declarations.
1385	///
1386	/// Two callee shapes are recognised:
1387	///
1388	///   * `llvm::FuncOp` — already lowered. We return its `arg_types()` directly.
1389	///     This covers device-extern declarations (inserted by the importer
1390	///     ahead of conversion) and any callee whose conversion happened to run
1391	///     before the current call.
1392	///
1393	///   * `MirFuncOp` — still in MIR form. We run `convert_function_type` to
1394	///     project its declared MIR signature into the same LLVM-level
1395	///     (slice/struct-flattened) parameter list that the function lowerer
1396	///     will eventually emit. This guarantees the call site sees the exact
1397	///     same parameter types the callee will end up with — including the
1398	///     address space of every pointer parameter.
1399	///
1400	/// Returns `None` when neither form is present (e.g. the callee lives in a
1401	/// different module). The caller then falls back to its legacy coercion.
1402	fn find_callee_arg_types(
1403	    ctx: &mut Context,
1404	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
1405	    callee_ident: &pliron::identifier::Identifier,
1406	) -> Option<Vec<TypeHandle>> {
1407	    let block = op.deref(ctx).get_parent_block()?;
1408	    let func_op = block.deref(ctx).get_parent_op(ctx)?;
1409	    let module_op = func_op.deref(ctx).get_parent_op(ctx)?;
1410	
1411	    let region = module_op.deref(ctx).get_region(0);
1412	    let module_block = region.deref(ctx).iter(ctx).next()?;
1413	
1414	    // First pass: walk the module's top-level ops once and extract whichever
1415	    // declaration shape (LLVM or MIR) carries the matching symbol name.
1416	    // `Ptr<Operation>` is `Copy`, so we can collect candidates without
1417	    // borrowing the context past this loop.
1418	    let mut llvm_decl_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>> = None;
1419	    let mut mir_decl_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>> = None;
1420	    for existing_op in module_block.deref(ctx).iter(ctx) {
1421	        if let Some(existing_func) = Operation::get_op::<llvm::FuncOp>(existing_op, ctx)
1422	            && &existing_func.get_symbol_name(ctx) == callee_ident
1423	        {
1424	            llvm_decl_op = Some(existing_op);
1425	            break;
1426	        }
1427	        if let Some(existing_func) = MirFuncOp::wrap(ctx, existing_op)
1428	            && &existing_func.get_symbol_name(ctx) == callee_ident
1429	        {
1430	            mir_decl_op = Some(existing_op);
1431	            // Keep scanning — an `llvm::FuncOp` would still take precedence.
1432	        }
1433	    }
1434	
1435	    if let Some(existing_op) = llvm_decl_op {
1436	        let func = Operation::get_op::<llvm::FuncOp>(existing_op, ctx)?;
1437	        let func_ty = func.get_type(ctx);
1438	        return Some(func_ty.deref(ctx).arg_types().clone());
1439	    }
1440	
1441	    if let Some(existing_op) = mir_decl_op {
1442	        let func = MirFuncOp::wrap(ctx, existing_op)?;
1443	        let mir_func_ty = func.get_type(ctx);
1444	        // Match the callee's own boundary kind: a kernel callee (rare; not
1445	        // produced by Rust code today, but cheap to keep correct) keeps its
1446	        // params as byval, internal callees stay flattened. Reading
1447	        // `is_kernel_func` here keeps `find_callee_arg_types` consistent
1448	        // with what `lowering.rs::convert_func` would produce for the
1449	        // same callee.
1450	        let callee_is_kernel = is_kernel_func(ctx, existing_op);
1451	        let llvm_func_ty = convert_function_type(ctx, mir_func_ty, callee_is_kernel).ok()?;
1452	        return Some(llvm_func_ty.deref(ctx).arg_types().clone());
1453	    }
1454	
1455	    None
1456	}
1457	
1458	/// Resolve a device-extern symbol name by stripping the internal prefix.
1459	///
1460	/// When calling `#[device] extern "C"` functions, the macro-expanded Rust code
1461	/// calls symbols like `cuda_oxide_device_extern_<hash>_foo`. We strip the
1462	/// reserved prefix here so the emitted LLVM IR refers to the original symbol
1463	/// `foo` that the linked LTOIR provides.
1464	///
1465	/// # Limitations
1466	///
1467	/// This approach derives the original name by stripping the prefix, which means:
1468	/// - Custom `#[link_name = "..."]` attributes are NOT honored (edge case).
1469	/// - If the original function is `bar` but the user wrote `#[link_name = "custom"]`,
1470	///   we'd emit `bar`, not `custom`. Acceptable for current call sites.
1471	fn resolve_device_extern_symbol(callee_name: &str) -> String {
1472	    device_extern_base_name(callee_name)
1473	        .map(str::to_string)
1474	        .unwrap_or_else(|| callee_name.to_string())
1475	}

6	//! The `MirToLlvmConversion` op interface.
7	//!
8	//! Defined here (in `mir-lower`) rather than in `dialect-mir` so that the
9	//! `#[op_interface_impl]` blocks for MIR ops can live in this crate without
10	//! violating Rust's orphan rules: the trait is local, so we can implement
11	//! it for foreign types (`MirAddOp`, `MirSubOp`, etc.).
12	
13	use pliron::{
14	    context::Context,
15	    derive::op_interface,
16	    irbuild::dialect_conversion::{DialectConversionRewriter, OperandsInfo},
17	    op::Op,
18	    result::Result,
19	};
20	
21	/// Op interface for `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect lowering.
22	///
23	/// Every `dialect-mir` and `dialect-nvvm` op that the lowering pass can
24	/// handle implements this interface. The pass dispatches via
25	/// `op_cast::<dyn MirToLlvmConversion>` instead of a manual if-chain over
26	/// `OpId`.
27	///
28	/// Called by `DialectConversion::rewrite` via `op_cast`-based dispatch.
29	/// Each concrete op provides its own implementation (see
30	/// `convert/interface_impls.rs`). The implementation should use `rewriter`
31	/// to replace the current op with one or more LLVM dialect ops.
32	#[op_interface]
33	pub trait MirToLlvmConversion {
34	    /// Lower this operation to the LLVM dialect.
35	    fn convert(
36	        &self,
37	        ctx: &mut Context,
38	        rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
39	        operands_info: &OperandsInfo,
40	    ) -> Result<()>;
41	
42	    /// Verification hook (no-op — the underlying op verifiers are sufficient).
43	    fn verify(_op: &dyn Op, _ctx: &Context) -> Result<()>
44	    where
45	        Self: Sized,
46	    {
47	        Ok(())
48	    }
49	}
50

1	/*
2	 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.

5	
6	//! Shared state types for `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect lowering.
7	//!
8	//! The DialectConversion framework handles value mapping and block mapping
9	//! automatically. This module provides the CUDA-specific state types that
10	//! certain ops need during conversion.
11	
12	use rustc_hash::FxHashMap;
13	
14	use crate::LoweringOptions;
15	use pliron::context::Context;
16	
17	mod options_storage {
18	    pliron::dict_key!(LOWERING_OPTIONS_KEY, "cuda_oxide_mir_lower_options");
19	}
20	
21	/// Store the options for the active lowering pass in pliron's per-compilation
22	/// context. Conversion interfaces only receive the context, so this keeps
23	/// policy explicit without consulting process-global environment variables in
24	/// individual operation converters.
25	pub(crate) fn set_lowering_options(ctx: &mut Context, options: LoweringOptions) {
26	    if let Some(index) = ctx
27	        .aux_data_map
28	        .get(&*options_storage::LOWERING_OPTIONS_KEY)
29	        .copied()
30	    {
31	        ctx.aux_data[index] = Box::new(options);
32	    } else {
33	        let index = ctx.aux_data.insert(Box::new(options));
34	        ctx.aux_data_map
35	            .insert(options_storage::LOWERING_OPTIONS_KEY.clone(), index);
36	    }
37	}
38	
39	/// Read options for the active lowering pass.
40	///
41	/// The default preserves the historical behavior for callers that use the
42	/// original `lower_mir_to_llvm` entry point.
43	pub(crate) fn lowering_options(ctx: &Context) -> LoweringOptions {
44	    ctx.aux_data_map
45	        .get(&*options_storage::LOWERING_OPTIONS_KEY)
46	        .and_then(|index| ctx.aux_data[*index].downcast_ref::<LoweringOptions>())
47	        .copied()
48	        .unwrap_or_default()
49	}
50	
51	/// Map from shared memory allocation keys to their LLVM global symbol names.
52	///
53	/// In CUDA kernels, shared memory is declared as module-level globals with
54	/// address space 3. When multiple operations reference the same shared allocation
55	/// (identified by a key string), they should all refer to the same global.
56	pub type SharedGlobalsMap = FxHashMap<String, pliron::identifier::Identifier>;
57	
58	/// Map from ordinary device static keys to LLVM global symbol names.
59	///
60	/// Ordinary Rust `static` / `static mut` values used from device code live in
61	/// CUDA global memory (address space 1), not shared memory.
62	pub type DeviceGlobalsMap = FxHashMap<String, pliron::identifier::Identifier>;
63	
64	/// Tracking for dynamic shared memory alignment per lowered function.
65	///
66	/// Maps function name to `(symbol_name, max_alignment)`.
67	///
68	/// Each function that owns a dynamic shared-memory access gets a symbol. Before
69	/// conversion, the pass combines the alignment requested by the function body
70	/// with every propagated launch-contract marker that can reach it. This
71	/// ensures a helper shared by several kernels uses their strongest requirement.
72	pub type DynamicSmemAlignmentMap = FxHashMap<String, (pliron::identifier::Identifier, u64)>;
73

1	/*
2	 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
3	 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	 */
5	
6	// Index-based loops are used intentionally for parallel array iteration patterns
7	#![allow(clippy::needless_range_loop)]
8	
9	//! # `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect Lowering
10	//!
11	//! This crate implements the lowering pass that converts
12	//! [`dialect-mir`][dialect_mir] operations into LLVM dialect operations
13	//! (provided by `pliron-llvm`, re-exported via [`llvm_export`]), with
14	//! GPU-specific operations lowered to inline PTX assembly or NVVM
15	//! intrinsic calls.
16	//!
17	//! ## Overview
18	//!
19	//! `mir-lower` bridges cuda-oxide's Rust-semantic dialect (`dialect-mir`)
20	//! to the LLVM dialect. After lowering, ordinary PTX builds go directly to
21	//! `llvm-export`. NVVM builds first pass through `nvvm-transforms`, which
22	//! adjusts the LLVM module for the selected libNVVM dialect.
23	//!
24	//! ## Compilation Pipeline Position
25	//!
26	//! ```text
27	//! Rust Source Code
28	//!        │
29	//!        ▼
30	//! ┌──────────────┐
31	//! │   rustc      │  (extracts Stable MIR)
32	//! └──────┬───────┘
33	//!        │
34	//!        ▼
35	//! ┌──────────────┐
36	//! │ mir-importer │  (Stable MIR → dialect-mir, mem2reg, annotated unroll)
37	//! └──────┬───────┘
38	//!        │
39	//!        ▼
40	//! ┌──────────────┐
41	//! │  mir-lower   │  ◄── THIS CRATE (dialect-mir → LLVM dialect)
42	//! └──────┬───────┘
43	//!        │
44	//!        ▼
45	//! ┌──────────────┐
46	//! │ llvm-export  │  (exports to LLVM IR)
47	//! └──────┬───────┘
48	//!        │
49	//!        ▼
50	//! ┌──────────────┐
51	//! │     llc      │  (LLVM IR → PTX)
52	//! └──────────────┘
53	//! ```
54	//!
55	//! ## Architecture
56	//!
57	//! The crate uses pliron's `DialectConversion` framework for the lowering
58	//! pass. The framework handles IR walking, def-before-use ordering, type
59	//! conversion, and block argument patching automatically. Each
60	//! `dialect-mir` / `dialect-nvvm` op declares its own conversion via the
61	//! `MirToLlvmConversion` op interface.
62	//!
63	//! ### Core Modules
64	//!
65	//! - **[`conversion_interface`]**: The `MirToLlvmConversion` op interface
66	//!   trait. Each `dialect-mir` / `dialect-nvvm` op implements this to
67	//!   declare how it lowers to the LLVM dialect.
68	//!
69	//! - **[`context`]**: CUDA-specific state maps (`SharedGlobalsMap`,
70	//!   `DynamicSmemAlignmentMap`) used during conversion.
71	//!
72	//! - **[`helpers`]**: Utility functions for creating LLVM dialect
73	//!   constants, declaring intrinsics, and navigating the IR hierarchy.
74	//!
75	//! ### Conversion Modules ([`convert`])
76	//!
77	//! - **[`convert::types`]**: Type conversion from `dialect-mir` types to
78	//!   LLVM dialect types.
79	//!
80	//! - **[`convert::ops`]**: Operation converters organized by semantic category:
81	//!   - `arithmetic` - Binary/unary math operations
82	//!   - `memory` - Load, store, alloca, pointer arithmetic
83	//!   - `control_flow` - Branch, return, assert
84	//!   - `constants` - Integer and float constants
85	//!   - `cast` - Type conversions (int↔float, widening, narrowing)
86	//!   - `aggregate` - Struct/tuple/enum operations
87	//!   - `call` - Function calls
88	//!
89	//! - **[`convert::intrinsics`]**: GPU intrinsic converters:
90	//!   - `basic` - Thread/block IDs, barrier
91	//!   - `warp` - Shuffle, vote operations
92	//!   - `mbarrier` - Asynchronous barriers (Hopper+)
93	//!   - `tma` - Tensor Memory Accelerator (Hopper+)
94	//!   - `wgmma` - Warpgroup Matrix Multiply-Accumulate (Hopper)
95	//!   - `tcgen05` - 5th-gen Tensor Core (Blackwell)
96	//!   - `stmatrix` - Shared memory matrix store
97	//!
98	//! ## Usage
99	//!
100	//! ```ignore
101	//! use mir_lower::lower_mir_to_llvm;
102	//! use pliron::context::Context;
103	//!
104	//! let mut ctx = Context::new();
105	//! // ... register dialects, translate MIR into dialect-mir ...
106	//!
107	//! lower_mir_to_llvm(&mut ctx, module_op)?;
108	//!
109	//! // module_op now contains LLVM dialect operations
110	//! ```
111	//!
112	//! ## GPU Intrinsic Lowering Strategy
113	//!
114	//! GPU intrinsics are lowered using two strategies:
115	//!
116	//! 1. **LLVM Intrinsic Calls**: For operations with direct NVVM intrinsic
117	//!    equivalents (e.g., `llvm_nvvm_read_ptx_sreg_tid_x` for thread ID).
118	//!
119	//! 2. **Inline PTX Assembly**: For complex operations without direct intrinsics,
120	//!    or where inline PTX provides better control (e.g., tcgen05, wgmma MMA).
121	//!    Uses `llvm.inlineasm` with the `convergent` attribute for warp-synchronous
122	//!    semantics.
123	
124	#![warn(missing_docs)]
125	
126	pub mod context;
127	pub mod conversion_interface;
128	pub mod convert;
129	pub mod helpers;
130	pub mod lowering;
131	pub mod type_conversion_interface;
132	
133	use rustc_hash::FxHashMap;
134	
135	use pliron::{
136	    builtin::types::{IntegerType, Signedness},
137	    context::{Context, Ptr},
138	    irbuild::dialect_conversion::{
139	        DialectConversion, DialectConversionRewriter, OperandsInfo, apply_dialect_conversion,
140	    },
141	    location::Located,
142	    op::{Op, op_cast},
143	    operation::Operation,
144	    result::Result,
145	    r#type::{TypeHandle, Typed, type_impls},
146	};
147	
148	use context::{DeviceGlobalsMap, DynamicSmemAlignmentMap, SharedGlobalsMap};
149	use conversion_interface::MirToLlvmConversion as MirToLlvmConversionInterface;
150	use convert::types::convert_type;
151	use type_conversion_interface::MirConvertibleType;
152	
153	// ============================================================================
154	// DialectConversion driver
155	// ============================================================================
156	
157	/// Options controlling the `dialect-mir` to LLVM dialect lowering pass.
158	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
159	pub struct LoweringOptions {
160	    /// Whether ordinary floating-point multiply/add or multiply/subtract
161	    /// expressions may contract into fused operations.
162	    ///
163	    /// This does not affect explicit fused operations such as `f32::mul_add`.
164	    pub allow_fma_contraction: bool,
165	}
166	
167	impl Default for LoweringOptions {
168	    fn default() -> Self {
169	        Self {
170	            allow_fma_contraction: true,
171	        }
172	    }
173	}
174	
175	/// `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect conversion driver.
176	///
177	/// Implements pliron's `DialectConversion` trait. The `rewrite` method uses
178	/// `op_cast`-based dispatch via the `MirToLlvmConversion` op interface,
179	/// so each `dialect-mir` / `dialect-nvvm` op declares its own lowering.
180	///
181	/// Holds CUDA-specific state that certain ops need during conversion:
182	/// shared-memory global deduplication and dynamic shared-memory alignment.
183	pub struct MirToLlvmConversionDriver {
184	    /// Shared memory global deduplication across all functions.
185	    pub shared_globals: SharedGlobalsMap,
186	    /// Device global deduplication across all functions.
187	    pub device_globals: DeviceGlobalsMap,
188	    /// Per-owning-function dynamic shared memory alignment tracking.
189	    pub dynamic_smem_alignments: DynamicSmemAlignmentMap,
190	}
191	
192	fn is_mir_or_nvvm_op(ctx: &Context, op: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
193	    let opid = Operation::get_opid(op, ctx);
194	    let dialect = opid.dialect.to_string();
195	    dialect == "mir" || dialect == "nvvm"
196	}
197	
198	/// True for a `builtin.constant` whose result is a signed/unsigned (non-signless)
199	/// integer. That is the only `builtin.constant` lowering must touch: `sccp` can
200	/// materialise such a constant (it carries the MIR integer type, e.g. `ui32`), and
201	/// lowering must normalise it to a signless LLVM integer like it does for
202	/// `mir.constant`, else the LLVM module ends up with mismatched operand types
203	/// (a signless op fed by a signed/unsigned constant). A *signless* builtin.constant
204	/// is left alone (legal; the textual exporter emits it). Because the conversion
205	/// emits a signless constant, this predicate is false for the result, so the
206	/// DialectConversion worklist converges instead of looping.
207	fn is_signed_builtin_constant(ctx: &Context, op: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
208	    if Operation::get_opid(op, ctx) != pliron::builtin::ops::ConstantOp::get_opid_static() {
209	        return false;
210	    }
211	    let res_ty = op.deref(ctx).get_result(0).get_type(ctx);
212	    res_ty
213	        .deref(ctx)
214	        .downcast_ref::<IntegerType>()
215	        .is_some_and(|it| it.signedness() != Signedness::Signless)
216	}
217	
218	impl DialectConversion for MirToLlvmConversionDriver {
219	    fn can_convert_op(&self, ctx: &Context, op: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
220	        // A signless `builtin.constant` is left alone: it is legal and the textual
221	        // exporter emits it directly. But sccp can materialise a builtin.constant
222	        // carrying a signed/unsigned MIR integer type; lowering must normalise that
223	        // to signless (like `mir.constant`), or the LLVM module gets mismatched
224	        // operand types. So mark ONLY a non-signless builtin.constant convertible.
225	        // Its conversion emits a signless constant (no longer convertible), so this
226	        // converges — it does NOT loop the way marking *every* builtin.constant did.
227	        is_mir_or_nvvm_op(ctx, op) || is_signed_builtin_constant(ctx, op)
228	    }
229	
230	    fn can_convert_type(&self, ctx: &Context, ty: TypeHandle) -> bool {
231	        let ty_ref = ty.deref(ctx);
232	
233	        // Signed/unsigned integers need signless normalisation (LLVM convention).
234	        if let Some(int_ty) = ty_ref.downcast_ref::<IntegerType>() {
235	            return int_ty.signedness() != Signedness::Signless;
236	        }
237	
238	        type_impls::<dyn MirConvertibleType>(&*ty_ref)
239	    }
240	
241	    fn convert_type(&mut self, ctx: &mut Context, ty: TypeHandle) -> Result<TypeHandle> {
242	        convert_type(ctx, ty).map_err(|e| pliron::input_error_noloc!("{e}"))
243	    }
244	
245	    fn rewrite(
246	        &mut self,
247	        ctx: &mut Context,
248	        rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
249	        op: Ptr<Operation>,
250	        operands_info: &OperandsInfo,
251	    ) -> Result<()> {
252	        let opid = Operation::get_opid(op, ctx);
253	        let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
254	
255	        // Special-case ops that need CUDA pass-level state.
256	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirFuncOp::get_opid_static() {
257	            return lowering::convert_func(
258	                ctx,
259	                rewriter,
260	                op,
261	                operands_info,
262	                &mut self.shared_globals,
263	                &mut self.dynamic_smem_alignments,
264	            );
265	        }
266	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirSharedAllocOp::get_opid_static() {
267	            return convert::ops::memory::convert_shared_alloc_dc(
268	                ctx,
269	                rewriter,
270	                op,
271	                operands_info,
272	                &mut self.shared_globals,
273	            );
274	        }
275	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirGlobalAllocOp::get_opid_static() {
276	            return convert::ops::memory::convert_global_alloc_dc(
277	                ctx,
278	                rewriter,
279	                op,
280	                operands_info,
281	                &mut self.device_globals,
282	            );
283	        }
284	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirExternSharedOp::get_opid_static() {
285	            return convert::ops::memory::convert_extern_shared_dc(
286	                ctx,
287	                rewriter,
288	                op,
289	                operands_info,
290	                &mut self.shared_globals,
291	                &mut self.dynamic_smem_alignments,
292	            );
293	        }
294	
295	        // Generic dispatch for all other ops via op_cast.
296	        let op_obj = Operation::get_op_dyn(op, ctx);
297	        let Some(converter) = op_cast::<dyn MirToLlvmConversionInterface>(op_obj.as_ref()) else {
298	            return pliron::input_err!(
299	                loc,
300	                "Unsupported MIR/NVVM op for lowering: {}",
301	                Operation::get_opid(op, ctx)
302	            );
303	        };
304	        converter.convert(ctx, rewriter, operands_info)
305	    }
306	}
307	
308	/// Runs the `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect lowering pass on the given module.
309	///
310	/// This is the main entry point for the lowering pass. It uses pliron's
311	/// `DialectConversion` framework to walk the IR, convert types, and
312	/// dispatch per-op conversion logic.
313	///
314	/// # Arguments
315	///
316	/// * `ctx` - Mutable reference to the pliron context
317	/// * `module_op` - Pointer to the module operation to transform
318	///
319	/// # Returns
320	///
321	/// `Ok(())` if all operations were successfully converted.
322	pub fn lower_mir_to_llvm(ctx: &mut Context, module_op: Ptr<Operation>) -> Result<()> {
323	    lower_mir_to_llvm_with_options(ctx, module_op, LoweringOptions::default())
324	}
325	
326	/// Runs the `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect lowering pass with explicit options.
327	///
328	/// Use this entry point when the caller needs compilation-wide floating-point
329	/// policy such as disabling implicit FMA contraction.
330	pub fn lower_mir_to_llvm_with_options(
331	    ctx: &mut Context,
332	    module_op: Ptr<Operation>,
333	    options: LoweringOptions,
334	) -> Result<()> {
335	    context::set_lowering_options(ctx, options);
336	    // Dynamic shared-memory operations may live in device helpers. Compute
337	    // every kernel-to-helper requirement while the complete MIR call graph is
338	    // still available; function conversion removes that graph incrementally.
339	    lowering::propagate_kernel_dynamic_shared_alignments(ctx, module_op);
340	    let mut conversion = MirToLlvmConversionDriver {
341	        shared_globals: FxHashMap::default(),
342	        device_globals: FxHashMap::default(),
343	        dynamic_smem_alignments: FxHashMap::default(),
344	    };
345	    // pliron's DialectConversion now reports an IRStatus (Changed/Unchanged);
346	    // lowering only cares about success, so discard it.
347	    apply_dialect_conversion(ctx, &mut conversion, module_op)?;
348	    Ok(())
349	}
350	
351	/// Register the `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect lowering pass with a pliron context.
352	///
353	/// This is a placeholder for future pass manager integration.
354	/// Currently, the pass is invoked directly via [`lower_mir_to_llvm`].
355	pub fn register(_ctx: &mut Context) {
356	    // Placeholder for future pass manager integration
357	}
358

390	
391	/// Multiply one warp-distributed INT8 tile and add an i32 accumulator.
392	///
393	/// Together, the 32 lanes compute `D = A × B + C` for row-major `A` with
394	/// shape 16×32, column-major `B` with shape 32×8, and `C`/`D` with shape
395	/// 16×8. Each lane supplies its fragments in registers and receives four i32
396	/// result registers. The call itself does not access memory or act as a fence.
397	///
398	/// Each `a` and `b` register packs four two's-complement `s8` values from low
399	/// byte to high byte. In other words, logical element `i` is stored in bits
400	/// `(i % 4) * 8..(i % 4 + 1) * 8` of register `[i / 4]`.
401	///
402	/// For lane `lane`, let `group = lane / 4` and `thread = lane % 4`:
403	///
404	/// ```text
405	/// A element i=0,...,15:
406	///   row = group       for 0 <= i < 4 or 8 <= i < 12; otherwise group + 8
407	///   col = thread*4 + (i&3) + (if i >= 8 { 16 } else { 0 })
408	///
409	/// B element i=0,...,7:
410	///   row = thread*4 + (i&3) + (if i >= 4 { 16 } else { 0 })
411	///   col = group
412	///
413	/// C/D register i=0,...,3:
414	///   row = group + (if i >= 2 { 8 } else { 0 })
415	///   col = thread*2 + (i&1)
416	/// ```
417	///
418	/// This is the non-`.satfinite` form. Signed accumulator overflow wraps rather
419	/// than clamping to `i32::MIN` or `i32::MAX`.
420	///
421	/// # PTX
422	///
423	/// ```ptx
424	/// mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k32.row.col.s32.s8.s8.s32
425	///     {%d0, %d1, %d2, %d3},
426	///     {%a0, %a1, %a2, %a3},
427	///     {%b0, %b1},
428	///     {%c0, %c1, %c2, %c3};
429	/// ```
430	///
431	/// # Safety
432	///
433	/// - All 32 lanes must execute the same instruction with the same qualifiers.
434	///   Calling from divergent control flow, or after any lane has exited, is
435	///   undefined behavior.
436	/// - `c`, `a`, and `b` must contain the calling lane's fragments in the layout
437	///   above. A different layout computes a different matrix operation.
438	/// - Requires `sm_80+` and PTX ISA 7.0+. cuda-oxide selects both floors
439	///   automatically and rejects an explicit lower target.
440	#[inline(never)]
441	#[must_use]
442	pub unsafe fn mma_m16n8k32_s32_s8(c: [i32; 4], a: [u32; 4], b: [u32; 2]) -> [i32; 4] {
443	    let _ = (c, a, b);
444	    unreachable!("mma_m16n8k32_s32_s8 called outside CUDA kernel context")
445	}
